title: Pell Norm FAIL: falsifier with wrong I value and wrong declared chain-entry Pell norm
_primary_source_note: Intentional falsifier for validator testing (not a new claim), same Pell identity as the PASS fixtures grounded in Wildberger's Pell equation paper (Wildberger, 2010) and the Pythagorean tree (Barning, 1963).
_fixture_purpose: Falsifier: declares chain[1]=(5,2) with wrong pell_norm/I/type (should be pell_norm=1,I=-1,type=E; declares -1,1,H instead), and witness (3,2) with I=999 (should be 7). Both trip PN_IDEN. NOTE: result is deliberately 'PASS' internally because this validator's validate() short-circuits to skip all checking when result=='FAIL' -- matches the pattern used in certs [214] and [144].

witnesses.check: FALSIFIER: I should be 7 (P(1,2)=1-8=-7, I=-(-7)=7), declared 999

validation_checks.description: FALSIFIER: chain[1]=(5,2) declared pell_norm=-1/I=1/type=H, computed pell_norm=1/I=-1/type=E; witness (3,2) declared I=999, computed 7
(d,e) move itself is genuine M_B(2,1)=(5,2), unaffected by this fixture's defect
